task_completion.description: Google Earth Pro application is opened
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Google Earth Pro application window is visible on the desktop.

task_completion.description: Switched view from Earth to Mars
task_completion.visual_evidence: The globe texture changes to a reddish/orange color, or the planet selection dropdown in the toolbar indicates 'Mars' is selected.

task_completion.description: Entered search query or coordinates for Olympus Mons
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search sidebar contains 'Olympus Mons' or coordinates near '18.65, 226.2'.

task_completion.description: Olympus Mons structure is visible in the viewport
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large shield volcano structure with a distinct escarpment (cliff edges) is visible on the map.

task_completion.description: Zoomed in on the volcanic caldera
task_completion.visual_evidence: The central crater complex (caldera) of the volcano is clearly visible and occupies a significant portion of the viewport.

task_completion.description: Initiated saving the screenshot to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Image' dialog or file save window is visible, showing the filename 'olympus_mons' and destination '/home/ga/Documents'.

integrity.description: Agent used the Google Earth GUI for navigation
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are used to send coordinates or control the application programmatically.

integrity.description: Agent did not download an image from the web
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not open a web browser to search for and download an existing image of Olympus Mons.

integrity.description: Image is rendered within the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The view of Mars includes Google Earth Pro UI elements (compass, scale, sidebar) verifying it is being rendered live, not just viewing a static image file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target: Olympus Mons on Mars. Coordinates: 18.65°N, 226.2°E. Visual features: Large shield volcano with a distinct central caldera (complex crater). Expected output path: /home/ga/Documents/olympus_mons.png (or .jpg).
